content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    :    Jeanne                                          Date    :    7/7/80


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION        A-50-007-039
         Jay

Subject:  Advice letters--W.F. O'Keeffe/S.F. Taxpayers Assn.


This advice letter was answered by telephone. After my
conversation with Mr. O'Keeffe, and because of the activities
described in paragraph 4 of the advice letter as well as his
explanation of activities described in paragraph 6, I advised
him that his organization would be a multi-purpose recipient
committee when the pro rata amount of receipts or expenditures
totaled $500 or more in a calendar year. TA materials were
sent to Mr. O'Keeffe.

W. F. O'KEEFFE, SR.
444 Corbett Avenue • San Francisco, California 94114
(415) 864-2344 • Message Phone 822-4222

3/21/80

F.P.P.C.
Political Reform Unit,
P.O. Box 1467,
Sacramento, Ca. 95807

Gentlemen:

Two months ago, I telephoned your office and described
our newly formed organization, the SAN FRANCISCO TAXPAYERS
ASSOCIATION". This is a non-profit corporate educational
organization dedicated solely to the reduction of the cost of
City government here in San Francisco. At the time, I was
told that such a non-profit educational organization would be
exempt from the jurisdiction of the F.P.P.C.

We were cautioned, however, that we would be precluded
from participating in any direct partisan political activity
with respect to candidates, but could endorse or oppose
individual local Charter amendments that affect the cost
of local government.

Not unlike the "League of Women Voters", we want to
provide the electorate with factual, statistical data on
the attendance, voting records, and tape recorded public
statements of existing members of our local Board of Supervisors
as well as other local officeholders.

We have now refined our proposed acrivities, as per the
attached summary "Purpose and proposed activities of the
"San Francisco Taxpayers Association", and wish to confirm
that the activities are exempt from F.P.P.C. filing requirements.

Obviously, our greatest concern is strict compliance
with the strictures of I.R.S. Bulletin #557, so that our
"100% tax deductible" status is unquestioned.

Inasmuch as our funding will originate from a broad spectrum
of local citizens, property owners, taxpayers, and business
concerns, and will not be limited to any specific "ceiling" on
donations and subscriptions, nor will the funds be identifiable
with any specific educational activity or ballot proposition,
it appears reasonable that we would be exempt from F.P.P.C.
regulations.

Much like a newspaper, we will likely offer editorial comments
in our monthly NEWSLETTER, and certainly additional unidentifiable
income will be generated from non-political advertising space
sold in the NEWSLETTER.

F.P.P.C.                    Page #2                    3/21/80


     Our legal counsel, Mr. James Stark, has suggested that
we have you review our proposed activities, as described in the
attached document, for compliance with your criteria for
exemption, and obtain written concurrence that the specific
activities we contemplate are considered exempt by you.

     If you note any activities that are non-conforming with
exempt status, please advise us immediately so that we
can make appropriate corrections.

                                        Yours truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        W. F. O'Keeffe, Sr. President.

                                        SAN FRANCISCO TAXPAYERS ASSOCIATION.

W. F. O'KEEFFE, SR.
444 Corbett Avenue • San Francisco, California 94114
(415) 864-2344 • Message Phone 822-4222

"SAN FRANCISCO TAXPAYERS ASSOCIATION"

PURPOSE:
    The fundemental purpose of the S.F.T.A., a non-profit,
tax exempt educational Corporation, is to minimize increases
in the San Francisco tax rate, discourage the imposition of
excessive new "fees" and "charges", influence the establishment
of appropriate priorities in the City's allocation of available
ad-valorum tax funds, and to significantly increase the efficiency
and effectiveness of Municipal Government in the City and County
of San Francisco.

PROPOSED ACTIVITIES:

    1. Provide individual members of the S.F.T.A. with a
condensed monthly NEWSLETTER describing all significant issues
that have been legislated or proposed during the previous month
by the current Board of Supervisors, along with their individual
voting records on each issue. It is generally concieved that the
media coverage of many important local issues is most limited
in scope. Some major costly issues are ignored entirely, and
individual Supervisorial voting records are rarely disclosed.

    2. Publish excerpted transcripts of tape recorded statements
made by individual Board members during their deliberations,
so that S.F.T.A. members can effectively evaluate the political
philosophy and judgement of each Board member.

    3. Emphasis in the NEWSLETTER will be concentrated on those
Board decisions that result in significant increased costs to
the taxpayers of this City. The activities of Administrative
Boards and Commissions whose decisions have a major impact on
the increased cost of City Government will also be monitored in
detail. Particular attention will be paid to the anticipated
economic impact of the quasi-legislative decrees and overall
performance of the Planning Commission.

    4. Provide funds for the research, publication, and City
wide distribution of ballot arguments covering proposed provisions
to the City Charter that appear to have an adverse impact on
the City's tax rate, housing stock, economic opportunities, or
the long term economic growth of the City.

    Provide funds to retain competent, experienced legal
counsel to the S.F.T.A. to insure that the City's bureaucracy
complies precisely with the requirements of the City Charter.
When appropriate, institute litigation or seek injunctive
relief from legislative or administrative decisions that appear
to illegally increase the present cost of Government in this City.
The issues involved must be of major importance. No trivial
or "obstructionist" type litigation will be undertaken.

W. F. O'KEEFFE, SR.
444 Corbett Avenue • San Francisco, California 94114
(415) 864-2344 • Message Phone 822-4222


S.F.T.A.        Page #2.

     5. The S.F.T.A. is currently represented by Mr. James Stark,
former Assistant City Attorney, who acted as full time legal
counsel to the Board of Supervisors. He is particularly
knowledgeable with the requirements of the existing Charter.
While foolhardy for any single individual to try to "fight
City Hall", the shared cost of such "public interest" litigation
would be negligible when undertaken on behalf of the several
thousand members of the S.F.T.A.

     6. Provide funds for the research, publication, and
distribution of unbiased, factual statistical data on the
attendance and voting records of each incumbent Supervisor.
This accumulated information will be specifically directed
to the registered voters in each Supervisor's district, just prior
to a Supervisorial election. The partisan advocacy of specific
candidates, or their opponents will be prohibited. The
information circulated to constituents will be exclusively
of a statistical, informative, factual, and educational
nature.

     7. Employ the services of a qualified, experienced, full
time investigative news reporter to gather "in depth" provable
evidence of substantial waste and mismanagement in City Bureaus
and Departments. This task has been seriously neglected by
the two major local newspapers. This activity will be concentrated
on the operations of the Municipal Railway, Social Services
Department, and the Wastewater Management Program. If solid,
proveable, substantiated data is developed, it will be disseminated
immediately to the local media, as well as to all S.F.T.A.
members in the monthly NEWSLETTER.

     8. The scope of all S.F.T.A. activities will be limited to
those complying with the "100% tax deductible" constraints
of I.R.S. Bulletin #557. Contributions, without limitation,
which support non-profit educational activities or activities which
tend to reduce the cost of Government are 100% tax deductible
to the donor. In addition, the non-profit status of the S.F.T.A.
will provide access to bulk mailing privileges at only 3.1¢
per letter mailed.

     9. While staff employees will be compensated at reasonable
prevailing rates, the Directors of the S.F.T.A. will serve
solely as unpaid public spirited citizens. Their reward will
be the knowledge that a well informed electorate is vital
for the enhanced liveability and economic stability of the
City of San Francisco.

                                                    W. F. O'Keeffe, Sr.
                                                    President,
"SAN FRANCISCO TAXPAYERS ASSOCIATION"

sections.question_synthetic: The San Francisco Taxpayers Association, a newly formed non-profit educational organization, seeks written confirmation from the FPPC that its proposed activities—including publishing voter education materials about Board of Supervisors voting records, endorsing or opposing Charter amendments, and conducting government accountability research—are exempt from FPPC filing requirements under California Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry from an organization to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present. Document contains two parts: cover letter dated 3/21/80 and detailed attachment describing organizational purpose and proposed activities. Some text is faded/illegible in the document image, particularly in the proposed activities section. The letter references prior telephone advice and mentions strict compliance with IRS Bulletin 557 for tax-deductible status.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The San Francisco Taxpayers Association, a newly formed non-profit educational organization, seeks written confirmation from the FPPC that its proposed activities—including publishing voter education materials about Board of Supervisors voting records, endorsing or opposing Charter amendments, and conducting government accountability research—are exempt from FPPC filing requirements under California Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from W.F. O'Keeffe, President of the San Francisco Taxpayers Association, to the FPPC seeking written confirmation that the organization's proposed educational activities are exempt from FPPC regulations. The request includes a detailed summary of the organization's purpose and nine categories of proposed activities.
